title: Mika's Lost Teddy
synopsis: When Mika loses her favorite teddy bear at the park, her friends and family help her search everywhere. Through teamwork and kindness, they discover that sometimes the best adventures happen when we help each other.

pages.text: Mika loved her teddy bear more than anything. His name was Mr. Fuzzy. He had soft brown fur and a red bow tie. Mika took him everywhere she went.

illustration.description: A young girl with curly black hair and brown skin sitting on her bed, hugging a brown teddy bear with a red bow tie. Her bedroom is cozy with colorful toys scattered around. The lighting is warm and golden, creating a happy, safe feeling.
illustration.alt: Mika hugging her teddy bear Mr. Fuzzy in her colorful bedroom

pages.text: Today, Mika was going to the park with her mom. 'Can Mr. Fuzzy come too?' she asked. 'Of course,' said Mom with a smile. 'He loves the park as much as you do.'

illustration.description: Mika's mother, a tall woman with short natural hair and warm brown skin, smiling down at Mika who is holding Mr. Fuzzy. They're standing by the front door with bright sunlight streaming in. Both are wearing casual, colorful clothes.
illustration.alt: Mika and her mom getting ready to leave for the park

pages.text: At the park, Mika played on the swings. She went down the big slide. She climbed on the monkey bars. Mr. Fuzzy watched from the picnic table. But when it was time to go home, something was wrong. Mr. Fuzzy was gone!

illustration.description: A busy playground scene with diverse children playing on colorful equipment. Mika looks worried and is searching around an empty picnic table. The park has green grass, blue sky, and rainbow playground equipment. Other families of different backgrounds are enjoying the park.
illustration.alt: Mika looking worried at an empty picnic table where Mr. Fuzzy should be

pages.text: 'Mom! Mr. Fuzzy is missing!' cried Mika. Her eyes filled with tears. 'Don't worry, sweetie,' said Mom. 'We'll find him. Let's look together.' They searched under the slide. They looked by the swings. No Mr. Fuzzy anywhere.

illustration.description: Mika with teary eyes pointing at the empty space while her mom kneels beside her, looking concerned but determined. They're both searching around playground equipment. The colors are slightly muted to show the worried mood, but still vibrant.
illustration.alt: Mika crying while she and her mom search for Mr. Fuzzy

pages.text: Just then, Mika's friend Aiden ran over. 'Why do you look sad?' he asked. 'I lost my teddy bear,' Mika sniffled. 'I'll help you find him!' said Aiden. His little sister Emma toddled over too. 'Me help!' she said, clapping her hands.

illustration.description: A boy with freckles and red hair in a blue t-shirt running toward Mika, with a small toddler with pigtails following behind. Aiden looks eager to help, and Emma is clapping excitedly. The scene is bright and shows the friendship forming.
illustration.alt: Aiden and his little sister Emma offering to help Mika

pages.text: Soon, more friends joined the search. Carlos looked in the sandbox. Zara checked the seesaw. Mrs. Park, the playground keeper, helped too. 'Many hands make light work,' she said with a kind smile.

illustration.description: A diverse group of children and adults searching different areas of the playground. Carlos, a boy with dark hair, is digging in a sandbox. Zara, a girl wearing a colorful hijab, is looking around the seesaw. Mrs. Park, an elderly Asian woman with gray hair, is smiling encouragingly.
illustration.alt: Friends and Mrs. Park helping search the playground for Mr. Fuzzy

pages.text: Emma pointed to the big oak tree. 'Fuzzy up!' she giggled. Everyone looked up. There, stuck on a high branch, was something brown and fuzzy! 'That's him!' shouted Mika. 'But how do we get him down?'

illustration.description: Everyone looking up at a tall oak tree with green leaves. High up in the branches, Mr. Fuzzy is visible, looking small and stuck. The children are pointing upward with excited expressions. Sunlight filters through the leaves creating dappled light.
illustration.alt: The group looking up at Mr. Fuzzy stuck high in the oak tree

pages.text: 'I have an idea!' said Mrs. Park. She went to her shed and brought back a long pole with a hook. 'This is for getting stuck balls and toys,' she explained. 'Let's see if it works for teddy bears too!'

illustration.description: Mrs. Park carrying a long pole with a hook, walking confidently toward the tree. The children are gathered around her, looking hopeful and excited. Her expression is wise and helpful, and she's wearing practical work clothes.
illustration.alt: Mrs. Park bringing a long pole to rescue Mr. Fuzzy

pages.text: Mrs. Park carefully lifted the pole. She reached up, up, up toward Mr. Fuzzy. 'Almost there,' she said. Everyone held their breath. The hook touched the teddy bear. Slowly, gently, she pulled him free!

illustration.description: Mrs. Park stretching up with the long pole while all the children watch anxiously below. Mr. Fuzzy is just being touched by the hook. The scene is full of tension and hope, with everyone's eyes focused upward. Bright blue sky in background.
illustration.alt: Mrs. Park carefully using the pole to reach Mr. Fuzzy in the tree

pages.text: Mr. Fuzzy tumbled down safely into Mika's arms. 'You're okay!' she cried, hugging him tight. The teddy bear was a little dusty but perfectly fine. His red bow tie was even still straight!

illustration.description: Mika catching Mr. Fuzzy with joy and relief, hugging him close to her chest. Her face is beaming with happiness. The teddy bear looks slightly dusty but unharmed with his red bow tie still neat. Golden afternoon light surrounds them.
illustration.alt: Mika happily reunited with Mr. Fuzzy

pages.text: 'Thank you, everyone!' said Mika. 'You're the best friends ever!' Aiden grinned. 'That's what friends do,' he said. Emma clapped her hands. 'Fuzzy safe!' she cheered. Carlos and Zara gave Mika big hugs.

illustration.description: All the friends in a happy group hug around Mika who is still holding Mr. Fuzzy. Everyone has big smiles and bright, cheerful expressions. The playground in the background looks warm and welcoming. The colors are rich and joyful.
illustration.alt: All the friends celebrating together after finding Mr. Fuzzy

pages.text: As they walked home, Mika thought about her wonderful day. She had lost something important, but she had found something even better. She had the best friends in the whole world. And Mr. Fuzzy was safe in her arms where he belonged.

illustration.description: Mika and her mom walking home at sunset, with Mika contentedly holding Mr. Fuzzy. In the background, her friends are waving goodbye with their own families. The sky is painted in warm orange and pink colors, creating a peaceful, happy ending scene.
illustration.alt: Mika walking home happily with Mr. Fuzzy as friends wave goodbye

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What is the name of Mika's teddy bear?
questions.explanation: Mika's teddy bear is named Mr. Fuzzy, and he has a red bow tie.

questions.question: Where did they find Mr. Fuzzy?
questions.explanation: Emma spotted Mr. Fuzzy stuck high up in the branches of the big oak tree.

questions.question: Who helped get Mr. Fuzzy down from the tree?
questions.explanation: Mrs. Park, the playground keeper, used a long pole with a hook to rescue Mr. Fuzzy from the tree.

questions.question: What did Emma say when she saw Mr. Fuzzy in the tree?
questions.explanation: Little Emma pointed to the tree and said 'Fuzzy up!' when she spotted the teddy bear.

questions.question: How did Mika feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: Mika felt happy to have Mr. Fuzzy back and grateful for her wonderful friends who helped her.

pages.title: Complete the Story
pages.instructions: Fill in the missing words to complete these sentences from the story.

blanks.sentence: Mika loved her _____ bear more than anything.
His name was Mr. _____ and he had a red bow tie.
Emma pointed to the big _____ tree.

pages.title: Who Said What?
pages.instructions: Match each character with what they said in the story.

blanks.sentence: _____ said 'Me help!' while clapping her hands.
